subject: History
chapter: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

key_points: The Revolt of 1857 was a major uprising against British rule in India
The revolt began in Meerut when sepoys refused to use greased cartridges
Sepoys believed the cartridges were greased with cow and pig fat, hurting religious sentiments
The rebels marched to Delhi and declared Bahadur Shah Zafar as their leader
Important leaders of the revolt included Rani Lakshmibai, Nana Sahib, Kunwar Singh and Begum Hazrat Mahal
The revolt spread to several regions such as Delhi, Kanpur, Lucknow and Jhansi
The revolt failed due to lack of unity, poor coordination and stronger British military power
After the revolt, the British government ended the rule of the East India Company and took direct control of India
examples: Sepoys in Meerut refusing to use the new cartridges
Rani Lakshmibai leading the revolt in Jhansi
Nana Sahib organizing resistance in Kanpur
Kunwar Singh leading rebel forces in Bihar
formulas: Causes of revolt → Political causes + Economic causes + Military causes + Religious fears
Spread of revolt → Meerut → Delhi → Kanpur → Lucknow → Jhansi
Result of revolt → End of Company rule + Direct British Crown rule
exam_tips: Remember the main causes of the Revolt of 1857
Mention important leaders and the regions they led
Write the major changes introduced after the revolt
